# Team Member Survey — TimeHuddle Market Research

> Copy these questions into a Google Form. Each `---` is a new section break.

**Form title**: How Do You Experience Your Team's Daily Work?
**Form description**: We're building a team productivity tool and want to
understand how individual contributors actually experience daily work rituals,
visibility, and time tracking. This takes about 5–7 minutes. No right or wrong
answers — we want honest impressions.

---

## Section 1: About You

**Q1 — Role**
*Multiple choice*
- Software engineer / developer
- Designer
- Product manager
- QA / tester
- DevOps / infrastructure
- Other (please describe): ___

**Q2 — Team size**
*Multiple choice*
- Just me
- 2–5 people
- 6–15 people
- 16–50 people
- More than 50 people

**Q3 — Do you participate in daily standups?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes, every day
- Yes, a few times a week
- Occasionally
- No

---

## Section 2: Standups

**Q4 — How do you currently run standups?**
*Multiple choice — pick all that apply*
- Video call (Zoom, Meet, Teams)
- In-person
- Async in a Slack/Teams channel
- A dedicated standup tool (e.g. Geekbot, Standuply)
- We don't do standups
- Other: ___

**Q5 — What's the most frustrating thing about your current standup process?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q6 — How long does your standup typically take?**
*Multiple choice*
- Under 5 minutes
- 5–10 minutes
- 10–20 minutes
- More than 20 minutes

**Q7 — Before a standup, how much time do you spend figuring out what to say?**
*Multiple choice*
- None — I always know off the top of my head
- 1–2 minutes reviewing notes or tickets
- 5+ minutes actually pulling things together
- I often feel underprepared

**Q8 — If your standup tool automatically showed you what tickets you worked on
and how much time you logged yesterday — so you just had to review and edit
instead of recall it — how useful would that be?**
*Scale: 1 (not useful at all) → 5 (extremely useful)*

---

## Section 3: Visibility and Time Tracking

**Q9 — Do you track your time today? (clock in/out, timesheets, etc.)**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes, required by my employer
- Yes, I do it voluntarily
- No, but I wish I did
- No, and I don't see the need

**Q10 — Can your teammates easily see what you're working on right now?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes — we have a shared board or tool everyone checks
- Somewhat — people can ask but there's no shared view
- Not really — people mostly don't know unless I tell them
- No visibility at all

**Q11 — When you want to know what a teammate is working on, what do you do?**
*Long text — open ended*

---

## Section 4: AI and Automation

**Q12 — If an AI assistant drafted your standup update based on your actual
activity (tickets touched, time logged, messages sent) and you just had to
approve or edit it — would you use it?**
*Multiple choice*
- Yes, absolutely
- Probably yes
- Not sure
- Probably not — I prefer writing it myself
- No — I don't want AI involved in my work communication

**Q13 — What would have to be true for you to trust an AI-drafted standup?**
*Long text — open ended*

---

## Section 5: Anything Else

**Q14 — What tool or feature do you wish your team had that it doesn't?**
*Long text — open ended*

**Q15 — Any other thoughts you'd like to share?**
*Long text — optional*
